intro: The Department of Design and Construction first opened its doors in July 1996, consolidating a capital construction process that had previously been split among separate divisions in other City agencies, to become the City's primary capital construction project manager. DDC builds and renovates civic facilities such as firehouses, libraries, courthouses, and senior centers, and delivers roadway, sewer, and water-main infrastructure in all five boroughs; since its founding it has completed more than 5,000 projects with a combined portfolio value of $34 billion.
